volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 2.2.330c - Section 2.2.374b)

sections.label: Section 2.2.330c
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.331
sections.original: Réspice ad me et relínque egentem párasitum , Panégyris .
sections.translation: Attend to me , and leave alone that needy Parasite , Philumena .

sections.label: Section 2.2.332
sections.translation: Pinacium .

sections.label: Section 2.2.332b
sections.translation: That name my elders gave me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.333
sections.translation: What ’ s your business

sections.label: Section 2.2.333b
sections.translation: What ’ s my business , do you ask ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.333c
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t I ask it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.333d
sections.translation: What ’ s yours with me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.334
sections.original: Mein fastidis , propudiose ? eloquere propere , Pinacium .
sections.translation: Do you insult me , impudent fellow ? Answer me , this very instant , Pinacium .

sections.label: Section 2.2.335
sections.original: Iube me omittere igitur hos , qui retinent .
sections.translation: Bid those , then , to let me alone , who are detaining me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.335b
sections.translation: Who are detaining you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.335c
sections.translation: Do you ask me that ? A lassitude is in possession of all my limbs .

sections.label: Section 2.2.336b
sections.translation: Well , I know right well that it ’ s not in possession of your tongue .

sections.label: Section 2.2.337b
sections.translation: With such rapid speed have I been hastening from the harbour , for the sake of your own well-doing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.338b
sections.translation: Why , do you bring any good news ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.339
sections.original: Nimio † inparti multo tanto plus quam speras .
sections.translation: I bring more , by very much , than you expect .

sections.label: Section 2.2.339b
sections.translation: I ’ m saved , then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.original: At ego perii , quoi medullam lassitudo perbibit .
sections.translation: And I ’ m done for ; lassitude is drinking up my marrow apace .

sections.label: Section 2.2.341
sections.original: Quid ego , quoi misero medullam ventris percepit fames .
sections.translation: What , then , am I , the marrow of whose stomach , to my sorrow , famine has seized upon ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.342
sections.translation: Did you meet any one ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.342b
sections.translation: Many .

sections.label: Section 2.2.342c
sections.translation: But any man ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.342d
sections.translation: Very many ; but , of the many , not one a greater rascal than he is .

sections.label: Section 2.2.343b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.344
sections.original: iam dudum ego ístum patior dicere iniuste mihi .
sections.translation: I have been affronted already at his saying uncivil things to me . If you irritate me any further —

sections.label: Section 2.2.345b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you ’ ll be plaguy hungry to eat me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346
sections.original: Animum inducam , ut istuc verum te elocutum esse arbitrer .
sections.translation: I ’ ll cause you to know that assuredly you ’ ve said that with reason .

sections.label: Section 2.2.347
sections.original: Munditias volo fieri . ecferte huc scopas simulque harundinem ,
sections.translation: I wish everything to be made clean . ( Calls to the SERVANTS from the door .) Bring out here your brooms , and a reed as well , [*] that I may destroy all the labours of the spiders and their plaguy webs , and rout out all their looms .

sections.label: Section 2.2.349b
sections.translation: The poor things will be cold in future .

sections.label: Section 2.2.350
sections.original: Quid ? illos itidemne esse censes quasi te , cum veste unica ?
sections.translation: What ? Do you think that they are just like yourself , with only one coat ? Take this broom .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351b
sections.translation: I ’ ll take it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351c
sections.translation: This I ’ ll take myself . Do you sweep away there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351d
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.352
sections.original: Ecquis huc effert nassiternam cúm aqua ?
sections.translation: Will some one bring here a pail and water [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.352b
sections.translation: Really , this fellow ’ s playing the Aedile [*] without the vote of the public even .

sections.label: Section 2.2.353b
sections.translation: Come , do you quickly sweep the ground , and sprinkle before the house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.354b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.354c
sections.translation: It needs be done .

sections.label: Section 2.2.355
sections.original: ego hinc araneas de foribus deiciam et de pariete .
sections.translation: I ’ ll knock down the spider-webs there from the door and from the wall .

sections.label: Section 2.2.356
sections.translation: I ’ faith , a troublesome business , this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.356b
sections.translation: Still , I don ’ t at all understand what it means ; unless , perchance , some guests are about to come ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.357b
sections.translation: Do you spread the couches .

sections.label: Section 2.2.358
sections.translation: The beginning pleases me , about the couches .

sections.label: Section 2.2.358b
sections.translation: Others , you chop the billets ; others , you clean the fish which the fisherman has brought ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.360
sections.translation: take you down the gammon of bacon and the collar of brawn [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.360b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , this is a very sensible fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.2.361
sections.original: Non ecastor , ut ego opinor , satis erae morem geris .
sections.translation: By my troth , as I imagine , you haven ’ t quite minded the directions of your mistress .

sections.label: Section 2.2.362
sections.original: Immo res omnis relictas habeo prae quod tu velis .
sections.translation: Why , I ’ ve left all matters unattended to by reason of what you wished .

sections.label: Section 2.2.363
sections.original: Tum tu igitur , qua causa missus es ad portum , id expedi .
sections.translation: Then do you inform me upon that , on account of which you were sent to the harbour ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.364
sections.original: Dicam . postquam me misisti ad portum , cum luci simul ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . After , with the daybreak , you had sent me to the harbour ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.365
sections.original: commodum radiosus sese sol superabat ex mari .
sections.translation: the sun with its beams opportunely arose from out of the sea . While I was enquiring of the revenue officers whether any ship had arrived from Asia , and they were saying none had come , I beheld , in the meantime , a bark , than which I think I never saw a greater one . With a favouring breeze , and in full sail , it came into harbour .

sections.label: Section 2.2.370
sections.original: alius alium percontamur : quoiast navis ? quid vehit ?
sections.translation: We were enquiring one of another whose ship it was , and what it carried ? In the meantime I espied your husband and his servant Stichus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.372
sections.translation: Ha ! what ? Did you mention Epignomus ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.372b
sections.translation: Your husband

sections.label: Section 2.2.372c
sections.translation: and my own life .

sections.label: Section 2.2.373
sections.translation: He has arrived , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.2.373b
sections.translation: Did you see him yourself ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.373c
sections.translation: Yes , and with pleasure too .

sections.label: Section 2.2.374
sections.translation: He has brought a great amount of silver and gold .

sections.label: Section 2.2.374b
sections.translation: ’ Tis right cleverly done .
